# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

Deno.serve(async (req) => {
  try {
    // Request network permissions at runtime for maximum flexibility.
    // This allows fetching from any host specified by the user.
    await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });

    const urlParams = new URL(req.url).searchParams;
    const targetUrl = urlParams.get("url");

    if (!targetUrl) {
      return new Response("Missing 'url' query parameter.", { status: 400 });
    }

    // Validate the URL to prevent potential issues, though "net" permission
    // already allows arbitrary hosts. This is a basic sanity check.
    try {
      new URL(targetUrl);
    } catch (e) {
      return new Response("Invalid URL provided.", { status: 400 });
    }

    const response = await fetch(targetUrl);

    // If the content type is JSON, parse and re-stringify to ensure it's valid JSON
    // before sending. Otherwise, send as text.
    const contentType = response.headers.get("content-type");
    if (contentType && contentType.includes("application/json")) {
      const json = await response.json();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(json), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    } else {
      const text = await response.text();
      return new Response(text, {
        headers: { "Content-Type": contentType || "text/plain" },
      });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error fetching URL:", error);
    if (error instanceof Deno.errors.PermissionDenied) {
      return new Response("Network permission denied. Ensure the deploy environment allows 'net' permissions.", { status: 500 });
    }
    return new Response(`Failed to fetch content: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
});
